ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content: [ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)

[ ] (Plutarch) Read an [overview](https://kids.nationalgeographic.com/history/article/ancient-rome) of the history and everyday life of the Ancient Romans.
[ ] (Plutarch) Read "[6 Ways Julius Caesar Changed Rome and the World](https://www.historyhit.com/ways-julius-caesar-changed-rome-and-the-world/)."
[ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)

[ ] Read more about the [history of Greece](https://www.natgeokids.com/ie/discover/history/greece/10-facts-about-the-ancient-greeks/).
[ ] Watch [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wA6qjniZ5YU) about Sparta (lots of views of the landscape!).

[ ] (Plutarch) If Plutarch were alive today, what political or military leaders do you think he would write about? Write a modern version of Plutarch's Lives featuring one (or comparing two) of these modern leaders. 
[ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)
